href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/17\/what-is-bluesky-everything-to-know-about-the-app-trying-to-replace-twitter\/\">Bluesky<\/a>, the startup aiming to build a decentralized social network to take on Twitter\/X, says it has begun deploying new safety tooling to help moderate content on the network through automation. Although still in private beta, the company has already made headlines for issues around content moderation in recent months after it <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/06\/08\/blueskys-growing-pains-strain-relationship-with-its-black-community-moderation\/\">initially didn&#8217;t ban a member making death threats,<\/a> and later didn&#8217;t catch that some people were <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/07\/27\/bluesky-racism-slur-apology-feedback\/\">creating accounts with racial slurs in their usernames.<\/a><\/p>\n<p>Now, the company says through a <a href=\"https:\/\/bsky.app\/profile\/safety.bsky.app\/post\/3kfgf5dqiii2l\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">post<\/a> from the Bluesky Safety account that it&#8217;s launching &#8220;more advanced automated tooling&#8221; designed to flag content that violates its Community Guidelines. The flagged content can then be reviewed by Bluesky&#8217;s moderation team to make a final determination.<\/p>\n<p>&#8220;We\u2019ll iterate on this so that mods can review offensive content, spam, etc. without any user seeing it first,&#8221; the post noted.<\/p>\n<p>In addition, the company said it will also add back the ability for users to report their own posts for mislabeled content to help the moderation team fix incorrect labels. Users today can note if their posts contain adult content, for example, from the compose screen on Bluesky&#8217;s app. Until then, other accounts will be able to submit reports about wrong labels on users&#8217; behalf, it says.<\/p>\n<p>Bluesky also touted the launch of other new features including user lists (generic lists of users) and moderation lists (lists you create in order to mute or block many users at once). Plus, it&#8217;s added the ability to sync users&#8217; moderation preferences between devices and remove adult content labels from posts that don&#8217;t contain images.<\/p>\n<p>Beyond moderation, Bluesky is developing another feature that X already offers: the ability to control who can respond to your posts. For example, users will soon be able to limit replies to only people they follow or users on a certain list. This is similar to the existing X feature that lets users limit replies to people they follow, verified accounts, or only those accounts mentioned by name, in addition to the default of &#8220;everyone.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>Despite these changes, some Bluesky users are still advocating for the ability to set their accounts to private &#8212; a feature they have an increased need for after Bluesky announced it <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/x-rival-bluesky-hits-2m-users-saying-federation-coming-early-next-year\/\">would launch a public web interface<\/a> that would allow users without an invite to the network the ability to browse posts. That drove demand for a private, friends-only account type, similar to X, as many users didn&#8217;t want their posts available to the world. Others are asking for the ability to remove followers and urging Bluesky to fully ban accounts that violate the company&#8217;s guidelines.<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>Though many users have verbally expressed their interest in an alternative to X that prioritizes content moderation and user safety, one such competitor, Pebble, found that safety measures alone were not a selling point. The startup did not sufficiently grow its user base <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/24\/pebble-the-twitter-alternative-previously-known-as-t2-is-closing-down\/\">and shut down in October.<\/a><\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Bluesky, the startup aiming to build a decentralized social network to take on Twitter\/X, says it has begun deploying new safety tooling to help moderate content on the network through automation. Although still in private beta, the company has already made headlines for issues around content moderation in recent months after it initially didn&#8217;t ban [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":2414667,"featured_media":2591425,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"c8045456-dda9-3ac5-b5e7-dc1201747582","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-12-01T19:00:48Z","apple_news_api_id":"2f0d6bcb-a097-476b-8abb-6abb40ed5439","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-12-01T19:13:11Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/apple.news\/ALw1ry6CXR2uKu2q7QO1UOQ","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[577051039,577055593],"tags":[449557101,577083484,449553673,49818],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- nch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=49818"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=49818"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=49818"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=49818"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2634301,"date":"2023-12-01T10:30:10","date_gmt":"2023-12-01T18:30:10","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2634301"},"modified":"2023-12-01T10:10:28","modified_gmt":"2023-12-01T18:10:28","slug":"sample-seed-pitch-deck-scalestack","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/12\/01\/sample-seed-pitch-deck-scalestack\/","title":{"rendered":"Pitch Deck Teardown: Scalestack&#8217;s $1M AI sales tech seed deck"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\"><span class=\"featured__span-first-words\">AI is everywhere<\/span>, and predictably, sales teams are among the early adopters. So I wasn&#8217;t surprised to see <a href=\"https:\/\/scalestack.ai\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Scalestack<\/a> raise $1 million to help make sales teams&#8217; lives easier.<\/p>\n<p>So, as we&#8217;ve done every week for the past 18 months or so (I can&#8217;t believe this is going to be <a href=\"http:\/\/haje.pub\/pdt\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">the 75th pitch deck teardown<\/a>!), we&#8217;re going to put the deck Scalestack used to raise its round under the proverbial microscope and see what it got right and where it could have done better.<\/p>\n<p>Let&#8217;s go!<\/p>\n<hr \/>\n<p style=\"text-align: center;\"><em>We&#8217;re looking for more unique pitch decks to tear down, so if you want to submit your own, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2022\/04\/28\/submit-your-pitch-deck-for-review\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">here&#8217;s how you can do that<\/a>.<\/em><\/p>\n<hr \/>\n<h2>Slides in this deck<\/h2>\n<p>Scalestack closed its $1 million round with an 18-slide deck. The company left all the slides in, but redacted some revenue figures and MongoDB&#8217;s ROI from its product:<\/p>\n<ol>\n<li>Cover slide<\/li>\n<li>Team slide<\/li>\n<li>Summary\/traction slide<\/li>\n<li>Problem slide<\/li>\n<li>Problem impact slide 1<\/li>\n<li>Problem impact slide 2<\/li>\n<li>Problem impact slide 3<\/li>\n<li>Solution slide<\/li>\n<li>Traction slide<\/li>\n<li>\u00a0 Customer story slide 1<\/li>\n<li>\u00a0 Customer story slide 2<\/li>\n<li>\u00a0 How it works slide<\/li>\n<li>\u00a0 Product slide<\/li>\n<li>\u00a0 Market slide<\/li>\n<li>\u00a0 Competition slide<\/li>\n<li>\u00a0 Positioning slide<\/li>\n<li>\u00a0 Why now slide<\/li>\n<li>\u00a0 Closing slide<\/li>\n<\/ol>\n<h2>Three things to love<\/h2>\n<p>Over the past couple of months, I&#8217;ve been experimenting with building an AI-powered deck review tool. It gave this deck only a 67% chance of raising money (you can <a href=\"https:\/\/haje.pub\/ai-scalestack\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">see the full report here<\/a>). Of course, Scalestack <em>did<\/em> raise money, and it did a bunch of things extremely well, which shows that AI has a long way to go.<\/p>\n<p>Taking a closer look at Scalestack&#8217;s deck, I&#8217;m not surprised the company raised money. It has three big things going for it: a killer team, impressive traction, and a customer testimonial to die for.<\/p>\n<h3>A great team<\/h3>\n<p>A company&#8217;s team is its heart and soul. In the early days of a startup, when there might not be a substantial track record or extensive financial data, the team&#8217;s background and capabilities can be one of the most persuasive aspects of your pitch.<\/p>\n<p>It&#8217;s essential to present your team effectively and showcase why they are the right people to bring the business to success. That&#8217;s what Scalestack got right, and it cleverly starts the story with a team slide.<\/p>\n<p><div id=\"attachment_2634332\" style=\"width: 1034px\" class=\"wp-caption aligncenter\"><img fetchpriority=\"high\" decoding=\"async\" aria-describedby=\"caption-attachment-2634332\" class=\"breakout size-full wp-image-2634332\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-2-of-18.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"1024\" height=\"577\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-2-of-18.png 1200w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-2-of-18.png?resize=150,85 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-2-of-18.png?resize=300,169 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-2-of-18.png?resize=768,433 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-2-of-18.png?resize=680,383 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-2-of-18.png?resize=50,28 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px\" \/><p id=\"caption-attachment-2634332\" class=\"wp-caption-text\">[Side 2] It&#8217;s awesome to see this company come out the gate unapologetically. <strong>Image Credits<\/strong>: Scalestack<\/p><\/div>You know what investors love? Founders with proven track records. This slide shows that its team is well-connected in the startup world and has operational experience to boot.<\/p>\n<p>I&#8217;d have loved to see links to the founders&#8217; LinkedIn profiles as well, but this works beautifully nevertheless.<\/p>\n<p>Long-time readers might notice that this slide is missing something I often argue for on team slides: Founder-market fit. Nothing in these biographies screams &#8220;sales&#8221; or &#8220;AI&#8221; to me, so why am I so enthusiastic about this company? Well, that&#8217;s because it doesn&#8217;t matter when you click through to the next slide:<\/p>\n<h3>Traction for dayyyyssss<\/h3>\n<p>Your startup&#8217;s team is important, but traction will make up for all the sins you can imagine. It makes sense, then, that after a team slide that good, Scalestack follows up with a traction victory lap. The actual data is redacted, but you get the picture.<\/p>\n<p><div id=\"attachment_2634333\" style=\"width: 1034px\" class=\"wp-caption aligncenter\"><img decoding=\"async\" aria-describedby=\"caption-attachment-2634333\" class=\"breakout size-full wp-image-2634333\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-3-of-18.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"1024\" height=\"577\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-3-of-18.png 1200w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-3-of-18.png?resize=150,85 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-3-of-18.png?resize=300,169 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-3-of-18.png?resize=768,433 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-3-of-18.png?resize=680,383 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-3-of-18.png?resize=50,28 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px\" \/><p id=\"caption-attachment-2634333\" class=\"wp-caption-text\">[Slide 3] Hyper growth shows the beginnings of product-market fit. <strong>Image Credits<\/strong>: Scalestack<\/p><\/div>Even with redacted numbers, this slide tells a hell of a story: Marquee customers, growing ARR and a strong pipeline. That checks a lot of boxes. The team will probably have been able to raise money with just these two slides, which is why I don&#8217;t give them <em>that<\/em> hard of a time for missing some important parts. Would it have been better to include all the data investors would be looking for? Sure, but if you have traction and a solid team, you&#8217;re 90% of the way there.<\/p>\n<p>If you&#8217;re raising money, take a hard look at these two slides. That&#8217;s what you&#8217;re up against, even for a company raising &#8220;only&#8221; $1 million. Does your company look like that? If not, can you tell a story that has a similar refrain?<\/p>\n<p>And if you can, will you be able to end the story with something this powerful?<\/p>\n<h3>A hell of a customer testimonial<\/h3>\n<p>Let&#8217;s get something straight: Customer testimonials are, by and large, vanity metrics that don&#8217;t tell the story by themselves. However, they can make for powerful narrative elements.<\/p>\n<p><div id=\"attachment_2634339\" style=\"width: 1034px\" class=\"wp-caption aligncenter\"><img decoding=\"async\" aria-describedby=\"caption-attachment-2634339\" class=\"breakout size-full wp-image-2634339\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-11-of-18.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"1024\" height=\"577\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-11-of-18.png 1200w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-11-of-18.png?resize=150,85 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-11-of-18.png?resize=300,169 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-11-of-18.png?resize=768,433 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-11-of-18.png?resize=680,383 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/scalestack-pitch-deck-teardown-by-haje-slide-11-of-18.png?resize=50,28 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px\" \/><p id=\"caption-attachment-2634339\" class=\"wp-caption-text\">[Slide 11] This isn&#8217;t a testimonial as much as a &#8220;We made ourselves indispensable&#8221; slide. Absolutely baller. <strong>Image Credits<\/strong>: Scalestack<\/p><\/div>You&#8217;d better believe that if I were leading an investment in Scalestack, I&#8217;d want to have a chat with Mr. Underhill. If he&#8217;s just as enthusiastic about this company on the reference call as well, it&#8217;d go a long way to convincing me to invest.<\/p>\n<p>Is it all perfect, though? Well, no. Objectively, this is not a very good pitch deck, because it&#8217;s missing some major parts of the pitch. So in the rest of this teardown, we\u2019ll look at three things Scalestack could have improved or done differently, along with its full pitch deck!<\/p>\n<p><div class=\"extra-crunch-offer-container\"><\/div>","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>We&#8217;re going to put the deck Scalestack used to raise its $1M seed round under the microscope to see what it got right, and where it could have done ":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[]]}},{"id":2636611,"date":"2023-12-01T10:03:29","date_gmt":"2023-12-01T18:03:29","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2636611"},"modified":"2023-12-01T11:45:33","modified_gmt":"2023-12-01T19:45:33","slug":"x-says-it-will-chase-smb-ad-dollars-after-musks-go-f-yourself-comments-to-fleeing-advertisers","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/12\/01\/x-says-it-will-chase-smb-ad-dollars-after-musks-go-f-yourself-comments-to-fleeing-advertisers\/","title":{"rendered":"X says it will chase SMB ad dollars after Musk&#8217;s &#8216;go  f*** yourself&#8217; comments to fleeing advertisers"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">A new report by the <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/12\/01\/capsules-new-app-combines-ai-and-human-editors-to-curate-the-news\/\">Financial Times<\/a> says X will now turn to small and medium-sized advertisers to shore up revenue after the company&#8217;s owner Elon Musk alienated big brands fleeing X over antisemitic content by <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/29\/elon-musk-says-go-fuck-yourself-to-advertisers-leaving-x\/\">telling them they could go fuck themselves during an interview<\/a> at the New York Times DealBook Summit earlier this week. But while the FT paints a picture of a bright future for X, where catering to SMBs was always the goal, Musk himself warned only days ago that the loss of big advertisers would spell the end of X.<\/p>\n<p>Speaking at the event, Musk told interviewer Andrew Ross Sorkin &#8220;What this advertising boycott is going to do is it&#8217;s going to kill the company,&#8221; he said with a small nod to the audience. &#8220;And what the whole world will know is that those advertisers killed the company and we will document it in great detail.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>That&#8217;s a different story than the one emerging today where a company representative informed the FT that &#8220;small and medium businesses are a very significant engine that we have definitely underplayed for a long time&#8221; and that &#8220;it [was] always part of the plan \u2014 now we will go even further with it.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>Of course, that could just be spin. In reality, X doesn&#8217;t have any other choice but to proceed with SMB ad opportunities, as many of the large brands that have left X aren&#8217;t planning on returning. According to a report by <a href=\"https:\/\/www.nytimes.com\/2023\/11\/30\/technology\/elon-musk-dealbook-advertisers.html\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">The New York Times<\/a>, half a dozen marketing agencies the paper spoke to said they would not resume advertising on X and others said they had advised advertisers to stop posting on the platform, as well. Some even said their temporary advertising pauses on X would likely turn permanent. The Times additionally noted that some 200 advertisers had halted spending on the platform after Musk endorsed an antisemitic conspiracy with one of his impulsive replies to another X user. Brands including Apple, Disney, Comcast, IBM, Lionsgate, Warner Bros., Paramount, and others, including now, <a href=\"https:\/\/www.reuters.com\/business\/retail-consumer\/walmart-says-it-is-not-advertising-social-platform-x-2023-12-01\/?ref=biztoc.com\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Walmart<\/a>, a <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2021\/11\/22\/walmart-will-be-the-first-retailer-to-test-twitters-new-livestream-shopping-platform\/\">former Twitter partner on e-commerce initiatives<\/a>, have<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/20\/apple-to-pause-advertising-on-x-after-musk-endorses-antisemitic-conspiracy\/\"> stopped advertising on the platform.\u00a0<\/a><\/p>\n<p>Though Musk attempted to clear up confusion over his remarks in subsequent posts, and again during his DealBook interview, it&#8217;s fair to say the damage has been done &#8212; and this time, it could be lasting.<\/p>\n<p>Sorkin, however, pushed back at Musk&#8217;s claims that the world would see the end of X as the <em>advertisers<\/em>&#8216; fault. He pressed Musk to re-examine that sentiment, noting that the advertisers would likely say that Musk&#8217;s own actions were to blame because he said &#8220;inappropriate things&#8221; on the platform, which made the advertisers feel uncomfortable.<\/p>\n<p>&#8220;Tell it to Earth,&#8221; rebutted Musk. &#8220;Let&#8217;s see how Earth responds to that. We&#8217;ll both make our cases and we&#8217;ll see what the outcome is.&#8221;<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>But there still remains a question as to whether Musk would really let X fail, even if ad revenues decline to the point that X was unsustainable as a business on its own. As the world&#8217;s <a href=\"https:\/\/www.investopedia.com\/articles\/investing\/012715\/5-richest-people-world.asp\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">wealthiest person<\/a>, Sorkin pointed out that Musk has &#8220;enormous&#8221; resources to keep the company afloat if he chose to do so.<\/p>\n<p>To that, Musk&#8217;s response indicated that he might allow X to die.<\/p>\n<p>&#8220;I mean, if the company fails because of an advertiser boycott, it will fail because of an advertiser boycott. And that will be what bankrupt the company &#8212; and that&#8217;s what everyone on Earth will know&#8230;Then it will be gone. It will be gone because of an advertiser boycott,&#8221; he said.<\/p>\n<p>When again pressed about the brand safety issues, he added, &#8220;Tell it to the judge. The judge is the public.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>In other words, Musk was trying to flip the script and pressure the advertisers to return to X or risk a boycott of their own.<\/p>\n<p>But as the FT reports, X is already working on new sources of ad revenue, citing an earlier tie-up with marketing firm JumpCrew, which it will outsource some ad sales to, targeting SMBs. And the company told the FT this push to reach SMBs would be accelerated. The report also noted,<a href=\"https:\/\/www.cnn.com\/2023\/11\/20\/tech\/ad-execs-encourage-x-ceo-linda-yaccarino-to-quit\/index.html#:~:text=A%20%E2%80%9Cgroundswell%E2%80%9D%20of%20advertising%20executives,marketing%20industry%20veteran%20Lou%20Paskalis.\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"> as others have<\/a>, that X CEO Linda Yaccarino has been &#8220;bombarded with calls&#8221; urging her to quit to protect her reputation. But so far, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/30\/x-ceo-linda-yaccarino-publicly-backs-musk-after-he-says-fck-yourself-to-advertisers\/\">Yaccarino has been backing up Musk following his comments<\/a>, <a href=\"https:\/\/www.cnbc.com\/2023\/11\/30\/read-linda-yaccarinos-message-to-x-employees-about-musk-interview.html\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">including via an internal email<\/a>, reported by CNBC, where she noted &#8220;our principles do not have a price tag, nor will they be compromised \u2013 ever. And no matter how hard they try, we will not be distracted by sideline critics who don\u2019t understand our mission.&#8221;<\/p>\n<div class=\"embed breakout embed-oembed embed--techcrunch\">\n<blockquote class=\"wp-embedded-content\" data-secret=\"MZHPiW3I9i\"><p><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/20\/apple-to-pause-advertising-on-x-after-musk-endorses-antisemitic-conspiracy\/\">Apple, Disney, Comcast among X advertisers pausing spending after Musk h.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=1700"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=1700"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=1700"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=1700"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2636538,"date":"2023-12-01T09:30:56","date_gmt":"2023-12-01T17:30:56","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2636538"},"modified":"2023-12-01T10:12:42","modified_gmt":"2023-12-01T18:12:42","slug":"rover-spac-sale-price","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/12\/01\/rover-spac-sale-price\/","title":{"rendered":"Why Rover&#8217;s $2.3B sale price makes good sense"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\"><span class=\"featured__span-first-words\">Rover is going<\/span> private in a $2.3 billion, all-cash sale to Blackstone, the company announced earlier this week. The pet care\u2013focused company raised <a href=\"https:\/\/www.crunchbase.com\/organization\/rover-com\/company_financials\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">hundreds of millions of dollars<\/a> while private, through a Series G, and later <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2021\/02\/16\/inside-rover-and-moneylions-spac-led-public-debuts\/\">went public via a SPAC<\/a>. Notably, unlike a great many SPAC combinations, Rover is proving that blank-check companies are not merely a way to incinerate wealth.<\/p>\n<hr \/>\n<p style=\"text-align: center;\"><strong><em>The Exchange explores startups, markets and money. <\/em><\/strong><\/p>\n<p style=\"text-align: center;\"><strong><em>Read it <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/subscribe\/?tpcc=theexchange\">every morning on TechCrunch+<\/a> or get <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/newsletters\/?utm_source=internal&amp;utm_medium=WPunit\">The Exchange newsletter<\/a> every Saturday.<\/em><\/strong><\/p>\n<hr \/>\n<p>The former startup has a 30-day shopping provision built into its deal with Blackstone, meaning that other offers may come to the fore. However, with the private equity group paying a stiff premium for Rover shares \u2014 61% more than the company&#8217;s 90-day volume weighted average share price, <a href=\"https:\/\/www.blackstone.com\/news\/press\/rover-agrees-to-be-acquired-by-blackstone-in-2-3-billion-transaction\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">per a release<\/a> \u2014 that doesn&#8217;t sound too likely.<\/p>\n<p><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/tag\/the-exchange\/\"><img fetchpriority=\"high\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignright wp-image-2220369 size-medium\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/10\/exchange-banner-sq-orng-plus.jpg?w=300\" alt=\"\" width=\"300\" height=\"300\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/10\/exchange-banner-sq-orng-plus.jpg 900w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/10\/exchange-banner-sq-orng-plus.jpg?resize=150,150 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/10\/exchange-banner-sq-orng-plus.jpg?resize=300,300 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/10\/exchange-banner-sq-orng-plus.jpg?resize=768,768 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/10\/exchange-banner-sq-orng-plus.jpg?resize=680,680 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/10\/exchange-banner-sq-orng-plus.jpg?resize=32,32 32w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/10\/exchange-banner-sq-orng-plus.jpg?resize=50,50 50w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/10\/exchange-banner-sq-orng-plus.jpg?resize=64,64 64w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/10\/exchange-banner-sq-orng-plus.jpg?resize=96,96 96w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/10\/exchange-banner-sq-orng-plus.jpg?resize=128,128 128w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px\" \/><\/a>The Rover deal is expensive but it has some notable caveats that tell us quite a lot about the state of the market for tech, and tech-enabled, companies. I&#8217;ll bet you didn&#8217;t expect a pet-focused e-commerce marketplace to earn an 8.7x revenue run rate multiple in 2023!<\/p>\n<p>This morning I want to dig into why I think Blackstone is paying so much for Rover, what we can glean from that research for other startups, and why a select few SPAC&#8217;d public companies that are trading like literal dogs may be bargains for the right buyer.<\/p>\n<h2>That price tag isn&#8217;t unreasonable<\/h2>\n<p>Rover and Blackstone announced their transaction after we got the former <a href=\"https:\/\/investors.rover.com\/news-releases\/news-release-details\/rover-reports-third-quarter-2023-financial-results\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">company&#8217;s Q3 2023 results<\/a>, meaning that we have pretty up-to-date figures concerning its recent performance.<\/p>\n<p>Rover reported third-quarter revenues of $66.2 million, up 30% from the same period one year ago. The company also flipped to GAAP net income and told investors that it intended to buy back more of its own shares. It also <a href=\"https:\/\/www.investorsobserver.com\/news\/stock-update\/rover-group-inc-up-23-65-to-8-00-after-earnings-beat\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">beat guidance<\/a> in the quarter.<div class=\"extra-crunch-offer-container\"><\/div>","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>The Rover deal is expensive, but it has some notable caveats that tell us quite a lot about the state of the market for tech, and tech-enabled, companies.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":428363,"featured_media":2621031,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"2d6e9d10-1a36-38b5-8ae0-2e959cef7c2e","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"","apple_news_api_id":"","apple_news_api_modified_at":"","apple_news_api_revision":"","apple_news_api_share_url":"","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[20429],"tags":[577025428,29172,576765839],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[576796356],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- ":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[]]}},{"id":2636650,"date":"2023-12-01T09:30:45","date_gmt":"2023-12-01T17:30:45","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2636650"},"modified":"2023-12-01T10:40:29","modified_gmt":"2023-12-01T18:40:29","slug":"revenue-based-financing-heats-up-in-the-middle-east-as-flow48-attracts-25m-in-funding","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/12\/01\/revenue-based-financing-heats-up-in-the-middle-east-as-flow48-attracts-25m-in-funding\/","title":{"rendered":"Revenue-based financing heats up in the Middle East as Flow48 attracts $25M in funding"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">We\u2019ve seen how non-dilutive, revenue-based financing has risen up the priority ranks for companies as the era of ZIRP \u2014 as the Zero Interest Rate Period is now lovingly and somewhat longingly known \u2014 recedes into the distance, and securing venture capital funds becomes harder. An example of this is how U.S. companies in this vein are oft raising capital, such as Lighter Capital, with its new $130 million credit facility, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/08\/29\/lighter-capital-130m-revenue-based-financing-startups\/\">in August<\/a>.<\/p>\n<p>And in Europe, at least 18 (at last count) RBF startups have appeared, according to Dealroom data, pulling in an impressive $671 million in venture capital. And that\u2019s not to leave out U.S.-based firms such as Pipe, Capchase and Clearco, all launching on the continent.<\/p>\n<p>Against this background, <a href=\"https:\/\/www.flow48.com\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Flow48<\/a>, a UAE-based fintech, has now raised $25 million in pre-Series A funding, with an aim to push this business model into the SME space in the MENA region. The round is a mix of equity and debt, and investors include 212 VC (a Luxembourg-domiciled, Istanbul-based fund), Austria\u2019s Speedinvest, Daphni, Blockchain Founders Fund, Unpopular Ventures, Endeavor Catalyst and TLG, as well as angel investors including Scott Sandell from NEA.<\/p>\n<p>The move is savvy because there is a lot more beyond Revenue Based Financing that a MENA-focused fintech lending platform could do in the growing regional economy. The region is forecast to grow by 5.4% in 2022 (the fastest rate since 2016) and by 3.5% in 2023, according to the IMF. Furthermore, <a href=\"https:\/\/www.bain.com\/contentassets\/2b078686303045ffa1d1207130ab5d79\/bain_report__ecommerce_in_mena.pdf\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">according to Bain<\/a>, the e-commerce market in Saudi Arabia, UAE and Egypt is <a href=\"https:\/\/ahdb.org.uk\/trade-and-policy\/export-opps\/regions\/mena\/macroeconomics\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">projected<\/a> to grow by more than 50% to $33.3 billion in the next three years.<\/p>\n<p>Founder Idriss Al Rifai told me me that the company doesn\u2019t have many competitors per se in its core markets, though it does \u201chave similar companies in the U.S.\/ Europe such as Wayflier, Pipe.com or the likes of Uncapped or Silvr in France.\u201d<\/p>\n<p>\u201cHowever,\u201d he said the Middle East and its \u201cpeculiar ecosystem and weaknesses\u201d (his words) is ripe for RBF.<\/p>\n<p>You might say Al Rifai has some knowledge of those weaknesses. He was previously founder of Fetchr, a Dubai-based express, mail delivery and logistics services company which raised $77 million, and famously brought last-mile delivery to a region which rarely has any kind of formal address system. It achieved this by allowing users to geolocate the pick-up and drop-off points for their packages, using a smartphone\u2019s GPS location as the address.<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>Al Rifai thinks that his experience of having to solve those &#8220;last-mile&#8221; issues in the Middle East will be brought to bear in this new arena of revenue-based financing: \u201cThe level of comprehensiveness and accuracy of information in the Middle East is way different than the one we have in the U.S. or in Europe. Therefore our product is different in the sense that it is plugged into various sources of information and data.\u201d<\/p>\n<p>He is therefore hoping Flow48 will be able to assemble a robust view of the the financial strength of its SME clients, and to that end the firm has already completed a pilot of its platform between this year and last.<\/p>\n<p>Certainly, the funding gap for SMEs in emerging markets is well known, so the opportunity to bridge this could well work in Flow48\u2019s favor. It is already planning to expand into South Africa.<\/p>\n<p>However, as we\u2019ve seen in the U.S. and Europe, it\u2019s unlikely Flow48 will be the last company to launch such a product in the growing MENA region.<\/p>\n<p>FlapKap, a revenue-based financing platform, is <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2022\/10\/12\/flapkap-provides-revenue-based-financing-to-e-commerce-brands-in-mena-gets-3-6m-seed-funding\/\">already<\/a> servicing markets such as Egypt and the UAE, and raised $3.6 million in seed funding last year, for instance.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>We\u2019ve seen how non-dilutive, revenue-based financing has risen up the priority ranks for companies as the era of ZIRP \u2014 as the Zero Interest Rate Period is now lovingly and somewhat longingly known \u2014 recedes into the distance, and securing venture capital funds becomes harder. An example of this is how U.S. companies in this [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":12084669,"featured_media":2636656,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"b54cc26d-e7f3-31e5-823b-18fa9e8c14c1","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-12-01T17:30:50Z","apple_news_api_id":"c356d61a-27f4-417e-907e-302ffa775d39","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-12-01T18:39:00Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAg==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/apple.news\/Aw1bWGif0QX6QfjAv-nddOQ","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[577030453],"tags":[449554678,577211976,65605158],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[577040726],"yoast_head":"<!-- This site is optimized with the Yoast SEO Premium plugin v20.2.1 (Yoast SEO v20.2.1) - https:\/\/yoast.com\/wordpress\/plugins\/seo\/ -->\n<title>Revenue-based financing heats up in the Middle East as Flow48 attracts $25M in funding | TechCrunch<\/title>\n<meta name=\"description\" content=\"We\u2019ve seen how non-dilutive, revenue-based financing has risen up the priority ranks for companies as the era of ZIRP \u2014 as the Zero Interest Rate Period Against this background, Flow48, a UAE-based fintech, has now raised $25 million in Pre-Series A funding, with an aim to push this business model into the SME space in the MENA region. The round is a mix of equity and debt, and investors include 212 VC (a Luxembourg-domiciled, Istanbul-based fund), Austria\u2019s Speedinvest, Daphni, Blockchain Founders Fund, Unpopular Ventures, Endeavor Catalyst, and TLG, as well as angel investors including Scott Sandell from NEA.\" \/>\n<link rel=\"canonical\" href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/12\/01\/revenue-based-financing-heats-up-in-the-middle-east-as-flow48-attracts-25m-in-funding\/\" \/>\n<meta property=\"og:locale\" content=\"en_US\" \/>\n<meta property=\"og:type\" content=\"article\" \/>\n<meta property=\"og:title\" content=\"Revenue-based financing heats up in the Middle East as Flow48 attracts $25M in funding | TechCrunch\" \/>\n<meta property=\"og:description\" content=\"We\u2019ve seen how non-dilutive, revenue-based financing has risen up the priority ranks for companies as the era of ZIRP \u2014 as the Zero Interest Rate Period Against this background, Flow48, a UAE-based fintech, has now raised $25 million in Pre-Series A funding, with an aim to push this business model into the SME space in the MENA region. the era of ZIRP \u2014 as the Zero Interest Rate Period Against this background, Flow48, a UAE-based fintech, has now raised $25 million in Pre-Series A funding, with an aim to push this business model into the SME space in the MENA region. The round is a mix of equity and debt, and investors include 212 VC (a Luxembourg-domiciled, Istanbul-based fund), Austria\u2019s Speedinvest, Daphni, Blockchain Founders Fund, Unpopular Ventures, Endeavor Catalyst, and TLG, as well as angel investors including Scott Sandell from NEA.","canonical":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/12\/01\/revenue-based-financing-heats-up-in-the-middle-east-as-flow48-attracts-25m-in-funding\/","og_locale":"en_US","og_type":"article","og_title":"Revenue-based financing heats up in the Middle East as Flow48 attracts $25M in funding | TechCrunch","og_description":"We\u2019ve seen how non-dilutive, revenue-based financing has risen up the priority ranks for companies as the era of ZIRP \u2014 as the Zero Interest Rate Period Against this background, Flow48, a UAE-based fintech, has now raised $25 million in Pre-Series A funding, with an aim to push this business model into the SME space in the MENA region. At Large","cbDescription":"<p>Mike Butcher (M.B.E.) is Editor-at-large of TechCrunch. He has written for UK national newspapers and magazines and been named one of the most influential people in European technology by Wired UK. He has spoken at the World Economic Forum, Web Summit, and DLD. He has interviewed Tony Blair, Dmitry Medvedev, Kevin Spacey, Lily Cole, Pavel Durov, Jimmy Wales, and many other tech leaders and celebrities. Mike is a regular broadcaster, appearing on BBC News, Sky News, CNBC, Channel 4, Al Jazeera and Bloomberg. He has also advised UK Prime Ministers and the Mayor of London on tech startup policy, as well as being a judge on The Apprentice UK. GQ magazine named him one of the 100 Most Connected Men in the UK. He is the co-founder <a href=\\\"https:\/\/thepathfounder.substack.com\\\">ThePathfounder.com<\/a> newsletter; <a href=\\\"https:\/\/theeuropas2021.thepathfounder.com\/\\\">TheEuropas.com<\/a> (the Annual European Tech Startup Conference & Awards for 12 years); and the non-profits <a href=\\\"https:\/\/techfugees.com\/\\\">Techfugees.com<\/a>, <a href=\\\"https:\/\/techvets.co\/\\\">TechVets.co<\/a>, and <a href=\\\"https:\/\/coadec.com\/\\\">Coadec.com<\/a>. He was awarded an MBE in the Queen\u2019s Birthday Honours list in 2016 for services to the UK technology industry and journalism.<\/p>","cbAvatar":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2021\/01\/bvnoe2vldznlydpvkdfj.png.png","twitter":"mikebutcher","_links":{"self":[{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/tc\/v1\/users\/12084669"}],"collection":[{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/tc\/v1\/users"}]}}],"author":[{"id":12084669,"name":"Mike r-poiln3\">ays Microsoft is talking to partners about launching an <\/span><span class=\"css-1qaijid r-bcqeeo r-qvutc0 r-poiln3 r-b88u0q\">Xbox<\/span> <span class=\"css-1qaijid r-bcqeeo r-qvutc0 r-poiln3 r-b88u0q\">mobile<\/span><span class=\"css-1qaijid r-bcqeeo r-qvutc0 r-poiln3\"> store, as first reported by <a href=\"https:\/\/www.bnnbloomberg.ca\/xbox-talking-to-partners-for-mobile-store-ceo-spencer-says-1.2005610\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Bloomberg<\/a>. Spencer made the comment during an interview at the CCXP comics and entertainment convention in Brazil on Thursday. <\/span><\/p>\n<p><span class=\"css-1qaijid r-bcqeeo r-qvutc0 r-poiln3\">The mobile store would take on Apple and Google, and could launch sooner than later, as Spencer noted that he doesn&#8217;t believe the launch is &#8220;multiple years away.&#8221; <a href=\"https:\/\/www.ft.com\/content\/7707705e-b288-4531-b30d-7fa993325018\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Earlier reports<\/a> have suggested that the launch could come as soon as next year.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>\n<p>\u201cIt\u2019s an important part of our strategy and something we are actively working on today not only alone, but talking to other partners who\u2019d also like to see more choice for how they can monetize on the phone,&#8221; Spencer said during the interview, as noted by Bloomberg. &#8220;I don\u2019t think this is multiple years away, I think this is sooner than that.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>Although Microsoft&#8217;s plans for an Xbox mobile store have been public for quite some time now, the new comments from Spencer come as Microsoft officially owns Activision Blizzard. It&#8217;s worth noting that court documents have recently shown that Activision Blizzard <a href=\"https:\/\/www.theverge.com\/23981939\/activision-blizzard-king-project-boston-android-app-store-games-candy-crush\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">had plans to launch<\/a> its own app store on Android.<\/p>\n<p>Microsoft has said in <a href=\"https:\/\/assets.publishing.service.gov.uk\/media\/634e5d3dd3bf7f618d8f88d1\/Activision_-_Initial_Phase_2_submission_a.pdf\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">previous fillings<\/a> that one of the major reasons it wanted to acquire Activision Blizzard was to help build out its mobile gaming presence, as Activision Blizzard is behind numerous mobile hits like Candy Crush and Call of Duty Mobile. In October 2022, Microsoft&#8217;s filings with the CMA<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2022\/10\/19\/microsoft-looks-to-build-an-xbox-mobile-gaming-store-with-activision-and-king-content\/\">\u00a0revealed that it planned<\/a> to create a new \u201cXbox Mobile Platform\u201d that includes mobile games by Activision and King.<\/p>\n<p>Microsoft&#8217;s deal with Activision Blizzard <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/12\/microsofts-68-7b-activision-acquisition-clears-final-hurdle-as-uk-approves-restructured-deal\/#:~:text=It's%20official%3A%20Microsoft%20closes%20%2468.7,UK%20approves%20restructured%20deal%20%7C%20TechCrunch\">closed in October<\/a>, almost two years after it was announced, which likely led to the company having to delay its mobile store launch plans.<\/p>\n<p>\u201cWe\u2019ve talked about choice, and today on your mobile phones, you don\u2019t have choice,\u2019\u2019 Spencer said during the interview. \u201cTo make sure that Xbox is not only relevant today but for the next 10, 20 years, we\u2019re going to have to be strong across many screens.\u201d<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>As noted by Bloomberg, the company&#8217;s plans to foray into mobile stores would come during a challenging regulatory climate amid the ongoing battle between Epic Games and Apple. Although Apple won its<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/04\/24\/apple-wins-antitrust-court-battle-with-epic-games-appeals-court-rules\/\"> legal battle against Epic<\/a> in April, the game developer asked the Supreme Court to <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/09\/27\/epic-asks-the-supreme-court-to-weigh-in-on-its-beef-with-apple\/\">weigh in on the matter<\/a> in September and review whether Apple\u2019s software business violates federal antitrust laws.<\/p>\n<p>Separately, Apple has<a href=\"https:\/\/www.reuters.com\/legal\/transactional\/apple-files-legal-challenge-eus-digital-markets-act-2023-11-17\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"> filed a legal case against the European Commission&#8217;s<\/a> Digital Markets Act, which has the potential to force Apple to open its app store ecosystem.<\/p>\n<div class=\"embed breakout embed-oembed embed--techcrunch\">\n<blockquote class=\"wp-embedded-content\" data-secret=\"QR0fHEGLEl\"><p><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/12\/microsofts-68-7b-activision-acquisition-clears-final-hurdle-as-uk-approves-restructured-deal\/\">It&#8217;s official: Microsoft closes $68.7B Activision Blizzard acquisition as UK approves restructured deal<\/a><\/p><\/blockquote>\n<p><iframe class=\"wp-embedded-content\" sandbox=\"allow-scripts\" security=\"restricted\" style=\"position: absolute; clip: rect(1px, 1px, 1px, 1px);\" title=\"&#8220;It&#8217;s official: Microsoft closes $68.7B Activision Blizzard acquisition as UK approves restructured deal&#8221; &#8212; TechCrunch\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/12\/microsofts-68-7b-activision-acquisition-clears-final-hurdle-as-uk-approves-restructured-deal\/embed\/#?secret=IfvpdmA6Hc#?secret=QR0fHEGLEl\" data-secret=\"QR0fHEGLEl\" width=\"800\" height=\"450\" frameborder=\"0\" marginwidth=\"0\" marginheight=\"0\" scrolling=\"no\"><\/iframe><\/div>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Microsoft Gaming CEO Phil Spencer says Microsoft is talking to partners about launching an Xbox mobile store, as first reported by Bloomberg. Spencer made the comment during an interview at the CCXP comics and entertainment convention in Brazil on Thursday. The mobile store would take on Apple and Google, and could launch sooner than later, gaming is a behemoth in terms of annual revenue, and our gaming news coverage includes iOS, Android, mobile web and more, including companies like Netflix trying to get a foot in the door. We also report on the AAA gaming studios, which are increasingly a target for acquisitive giants like Microsoft and com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=502"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=502"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=502"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=502"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2636226,"date":"2023-12-01T09:00:42","date_gmt":"2023-12-01T17:00:42","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2636226"},"modified":"2023-12-01T09:08:53","modified_gmt":"2023-12-01T17:08:53","slug":"amazon-ai-catchup-microsoft","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/12\/01\/amazon-ai-catchup-microsoft\/","title":{"rendered":"Amazon finds itself in the unusual position of playing catch-up in AI"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\"><span class=\"featured__span-first-words\">It was kind<\/span> of a strange AWS re:Invent this week in Las Vegas. Once again, there wasn\u2019t a huge <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/29\/heres-everything-aws-reinvent-2023-so-far\/\">amount of news<\/a> coming out of the massive customer event; the biggest announcements were related to AI, of course. But the real story could be that Amazon has found itself in the uncomfortable position of chasing Microsoft when it comes to AI.<\/p>\n<p>And that\u2019s in spite of <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/29\/a-timeline-of-sam-altmans-firing-from-openai-and-the-fallout\/\">the drama that played out at OpenAI<\/a> last week, in which Microsoft was squarely in the middle.<\/p>\n<p>The rivalry became clear when Amazon CEO Adam Selipsky took some <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/28\/aws-takes-the-cheap-shots\/\">not-so-subtle cheap shots<\/a> at AWS\u2019 cloud rival during the main keynote on Tuesday.<\/p>\n<p>To be clear, being behind, if that\u2019s what\u2019s happening, is not necessarily fatal for Amazon. It has dominated the cloud since <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2016\/07\/02\/andy-jassys-brief-history-of-the-genesis-of-aws\/\">it invented the idea <\/a>in 2006. And the generative AI landscape is still so nascent, and the market is shifting so quickly, that the perception among investors and some in the media, that Microsoft is ahead, could be moot in 12 or 18 months.<\/p>\n<p>Like last year, the <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2022\/12\/02\/the-era-of-constant-innovation-at-amazon-could-be-over\/\">truly newsworthy announcements<\/a> were thin with perhaps the most interesting being <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/28\/amazon-unveils-q-an-ai-powered-chatbot-for-businesses\/\">Amazon Q<\/a>. It helps developers connect a generative AI layer to enterprise software and was being billed by some at the event as Amazon\u2019s answer to Microsoft Copilot. And that could give credence to the idea that the cloud giant is, in fact, playing catch-up here.<\/p>\n<p>Scott Raney, a partner at Redpoint, says that Microsoft made a couple of great moves, like buying <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2018\/10\/26\/microsoft-closes-its-7-5b-purchase-of-code-sharing-platform-github\/\">GitHub for $7.5 billion<\/a> in 2018 and investing <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/01\/23\/microsoft-invests-billions-more-dollars-in-openai-extends-partnership\/\">at least $10 billion in OpenAI<\/a>. That has put it in a good position to take advantage of the generative AI wave companies have been riding this year.<div class=\"extra-crunch-offer-container\"><\/div>","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>&#8220;This might be the first time where people looked and said that Amazon isn&#8217;t in the pole position to capitalize on this massive opportunity.&#8221;<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":521068,"featured_media":2636228,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"b26570f2-cdf8-39d9-aa9a-34ed088c19d8","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"","apple_news_api_id":"","apple_news_api_modified_at":"","apple_news_api_revision":"","apple_news_api_share_url":"","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[577047203,449557044],"tags":[6602,577210801,576830288,576717904,637],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[576796354],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- ":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[]]}},{"id":2636597,"date":"2023-12-01T08:20:20","date_gmt":"2023-12-01T16:20:20","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2636597"},"modified":"2023-12-01T10:16:21","modified_gmt":"2023-12-01T18:16:21","slug":"capsules-new-app-combines-ai-and-human-editors-to-curate-the-news","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/12\/01\/capsules-new-app-combines-ai-and-human-editors-to-curate-the-news\/","title":{"rendered":"Capsule&#8217;s new app combines AI and human editors to curate the news"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">A Paris-based startup called <a href=\"https:\/\/capsule.news\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Capsule<\/a> wants to transform the way users seek out their daily news. Instead of offering a standard news aggregator experience, Capsule&#8217;s mission is to become the &#8220;Spotify for news&#8221; by combining AI technology and human editorial curation to provide a news-reading experience that transforms news articles, newsletter snippets and even tweets (posts on X) and other social updates, into digestible information through its user-friendly app.<\/p>\n<p>The experience of using Capsule, meanwhile, feels much like swiping through TikTok or other social apps in some ways. The app employs a vertical scrolling feed where news is presented as a series of headlines with accompanying photos, or screenshots of posts on platforms like X and LinkedIn. You can tap on any of these headlines to read a summary of the news, then optionally tap the link to the news source in the upper-left corner to read the full article (or social post) directly on the publisher&#8217;s website.<\/p>\n<p>The idea for the app comes from co-founders J\u00e9r\u00f4me Bo\u00e9 and Arthur de Villemandy. Bo\u00e9 had previously developed a clever <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2021\/11\/18\/new-app-snax-combines-short-form-movies-with-interactive-games\/\">short-form video app called Snax<\/a>, which he later had to shut down due to the operational costs outweighing the returns, he says. Co-founder de Villemandy, meanwhile, had previously authored a newsletter called &#8220;Magma&#8221; which focused on business trends and insights.<\/p>\n<p><div id=\"attachment_2636603\" style=\"width: 690px\" class=\"wp-caption aligncenter\"><img fetchpriority=\"high\" decoding=\"async\" aria-describedby=\"caption-attachment-2636603\" class=\"size-large wp-image-2636603\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/Screenshot-2023-12-01-at-11.11.45\u202fAM.jpg?w=680\" alt=\"\" width=\"680\" height=\"363\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/Screenshot-2023-12-01-at-11.11.45\u202fAM.jpg 1962w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/Screenshot-2023-12-01-at-11.11.45\u202fAM.jpg?resize=150,80 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/Screenshot-2023-12-01-at-11.11.45\u202fAM.jpg?resize=300,160 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/Screenshot-2023-12-01-at-11.11.45\u202fAM.jpg?resize=768,409 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/Screenshot-2023-12-01-at-11.11.45\u202fAM.jpg?resize=680,363 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/Screenshot-2023-12-01-at-11.11.45\u202fAM.jpg?resize=1536,819 1536w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/Screenshot-2023-12-01-at-11.11.45\u202fAM.jpg?resize=1200,640 1200w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/Screenshot-2023-12-01-at-11.11.45\u202fAM.jpg?resize=50,27 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 680px) 100vw, 680px\" \/><p id=\"caption-attachment-2636603\" class=\"wp-caption-text\"><strong>Image Credits:<\/strong> Capsule<\/p><\/div><\/p>\n<p>Bo\u00e9 explains the inspiration for their new company was to find a way to make quality news more accessible by leaning on a network of curators. Later, the company plans to organize the world of news and information the way Spotify organizes music by creating &#8220;playlists&#8221; of news and adding recommendations.<\/p>\n<p>&#8220;Capsule isn&#8217;t trying to be another Google News-style aggregator,&#8221; Bo\u00e9 tells TechCrunch. &#8220;It&#8217;s more than just clicking through to a source. Think of it as a starting point where we dive deeper. We research, cross-reference, and inject extra data to give users a fuller picture. It&#8217;s like a mini newsletter, packed with clickable links, images and interactive elements are on the horizon,&#8221; he says.<\/p>\n<p>The company leverages AI to distill the essential information or the main insights from an article. Then, Capsule&#8217;s editorial team adds to these insights with additional research. Currently, Capsule employs a team of 10 freelancers from around the globe for this effort. These curators are described by the company as &#8220;avid readers with a unique perspective.&#8221; They also are tasked with seeding the app with new content, an area where Bo\u00e9 says &#8220;AI doesn&#8217;t cut it.&#8221;<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p><div id=\"attachment_2636607\" style=\"width: 324px\" class=\"wp-caption aligncenter\"><img decoding=\"async\" aria-describedby=\"caption-attachment-2636607\" class=\"vertical size-large wp-image-2636607\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/IMG_7579.png?w=314\" alt=\"\" width=\"314\" height=\"680\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/IMG_7579.png 1290w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/IMG_7579.png?resize=69,150 69w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/IMG_7579.png?resize=138,300 138w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/IMG_7579.png?resize=768,1665 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/IMG_7579.png?resize=314,680 314w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/IMG_7579.png?resize=709,1536 709w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/IMG_7579.png?resize=945,2048 945w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/IMG_7579.png?resize=554,1200 554w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/12\/IMG_7579.png?resize=23,50 23w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 314px) 100vw, 314px\" \/><p id=\"caption-attachment-2636607\" class=\"wp-caption-text\"><strong>Image Credits:<\/strong> Capsule<\/p><\/div><\/p>\n<p>&#8220;It needs a nuanced, subjective approach to see if something fits our editorial ethos which is all about trends and insights for modern leaders,&#8221; he explains. &#8220;We focus solely on the quality and relevance of the content, deliberately avoiding other metrics such as social shares or likes.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>The curators&#8217; news findings are also sourced and verified by Capsule&#8217;s editorial team, who then present the news in the app in a digestible format. Every day, there are around 20 to 30 selected insights added to Capsule, that highlight things like major news, strong trends and even weaker signals.<\/p>\n<p>AI is also used to help curate related news stories. Beneath articles in Capsule, users will find a selection of similar news. This helps to enhance the &#8220;depth and breadth&#8221; of their reading experience, says Bo\u00e9.<\/p>\n<p>Unlike other modern news-sharing apps, like Artifact, Capsule has no current limitations on the sources for the news that may show up in its feed. So far, the app has already tapped into over 400 news sources and counting, the company notes.<\/p>\n<p>For now, the four-person team at Capsule is bootstrapping the startup but is considering a freemium model in the future if Capsule is able to perfect the user experience and find traction, Bo\u00e9 says.<\/p>\n<p>The app is currently <a href=\"https:\/\/apps.apple.com\/us\/app\/capsule-news-to-stay-ahead\/id6444951354\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">a free download on iOS<\/a>.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>A Paris-based startup called Capsule wants to transform the way users seek out their daily news. Instead of offering a standard news aggregator experience, Capsule&#8217;s mission is to become the &#8220;Spotify for news&#8221; by combining AI technology and human editorial curation to provide a news-reading experience that transforms news articles, newsletter snippets and even tweets [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":2414667,"featured_media":1855070,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"bd733a9e-ec1c-3e44-a32b-fa7b17715721","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-12-01T16:20:28Z","apple_news_api_id":"5c88103e-f516-4531-9407-cab2a07eda7b","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-12-01T18:16:12Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231202000540\/https:\/\/apple.news\/AXIgQPvUWRTGUB8qyoH7aew","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[577047203,577051039,577030456],"tags":[449557101,448606636,103,1473],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- This site is optimized with the Yoast SEO Premium plugin v20.2.1 (Yoast SEO v20.2.1) - https:\/\/yoast.com\/wordpress\/plugins\/seo\/ -->\n<title>Capsule&#039;s new app combines AI and human editors to curate the news | TechCrunch<\/title>\n<meta name=\"description\" content=\"A Paris-based startup called Capsule wants to transform the way users seek out their daily news. Instead of offering a standard news aggregator\" \/>\n<link rel=\"canonical\" href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/12\/01\/capsules-new-app-combines-ai-and-human-editors-to-curate-the-news\/\" \/>\n<meta property=\"og:locale\" content=\"en_US\" \/>\n<meta property=\"og:type\" content=\"article\" \/>\n<meta property=\"og:title\" content=\"Capsule&#039;s new app combines AI and human editors to curate the news | TechCrunch\" \/>\n<meta property=\"og:description\" content=\"A Paris-based startup called Capsule wants to transform the way users seek out their daily news. Instead of offering a standard news aggregator\" \/>\n<meta property=\"og:url\" content=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/12\/01\/capsules-new-app-combines-ai-and-human-editors-to-curate-the-news\/\" \/>\n<meta property=\"og:site_name\" content=\"TechCrunch\" \/>\n<meta property=\"article:publisher\" content=\"https:\/\/www.facebook.com\/techcrunch\" \/>\n<meta property=\"article:published_time\"
